---
title: "LRP72base: Phonics-route baseline, no L x B interaction"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_gate_badge.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_key_findings.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_reading_guide.qmd >}}

## Overview

LRP72base is the no-interaction companion to [LRP72](../lrp-rli-mech-072/). It uses the
same outcome, data window and linear letter-sound mechanism, and enters phoneme
blending (`B`) through the **same** moderator main effect $\gamma_{\text{mod}} z(B)$ as
LRP72, with `include_interaction=False` dropping only the $\gamma_{\text{int}}$ ($L \times B$)
term. The two models therefore differ by *exactly* the interaction, with an identical
prior on the blending main effect — a clean nested comparison.

The purpose is model comparison: LRP72 versus LRP72base asks whether adding the
letter-sound x blending interaction improves predictive fit for nonword decoding.
All coefficients are adjusted associations, not causal skill-to-skill effects.

## Model

$$
\eta = \alpha + \alpha_{\text{phase}}
  + \beta_G G
  + \beta_{\text{mech}} z(\operatorname{logit} L_{\text{post}})
  + \gamma_{\text{mod}} z(B)
  + \gamma_{\text{own}}\operatorname{logit}_*(N_{\text{pre}})
  + \gamma_A A_{\text{std}}
  + u_{\text{child}}.
$$

The outcome is nonword decoding (`N`, `nonword`), with a Beta-Binomial likelihood.
The mechanism is linear because the 0-6 nonword outcome is low and floored; the
full HSGP mechanism is not identifiable for this comparator.

![Model diagram](model_graph.png){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_results_mechanism.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_technical.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
